mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series yields negative forward returns in greater than 60% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of contraction periods during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index combined with a stationary federal funds rate creates real negative interest rates, prompting investors to rotate into equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS change <= 0
failure_condition: Real negative rate environments produce negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observations over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains wage income and aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation that reduces equity risk premiums and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed 0% in greater than 60% of low-growth quarters during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates robust economic momentum that immediately boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases MoM while UNRATE decreases MoM
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of divergence instances during recovery phases.
